topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an irritating buzz, mirroring the frantic thumping in Maya’s chest. She stared at the acceptance email, the words 'Congratulations' feeling like a physical weight pressing down on her shoulders. Across the desk, Liam was already laughing, a bright, careless sound that grated on her nerves. He leaned back, a picture of effortless victory, while she meticulously smoothed the crease from her already perfectly ironed interview shirt. Every muscle in her jaw felt tight, a knot of sheer awkwardness refusing to loosen. She picked up her lukewarm coffee, the ceramic suddenly feeling slick in her clammy grip, and took a slow, deliberate sip, trying to anchor herself against the dizzying sense of being utterly overlooked.

[story 2]

"Well, that's settled then," Mark said, his tone breezy as he stacked the identical application packets on the corner of his desk. He offered a small, patronizing smile that didn't quite reach his eyes. Sarah watched him, the color draining slightly from her cheeks. She felt a strange, hollow sensation spreading through her core, like water draining from a punctured vessel. When he finally turned to leave, she felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to retreat, to simply melt into the beige cubicle wall. Her hands trembled slightly as she reached for her bag, the simple act of gathering her things feeling monumental, a performance she wasn't sure she had the energy for.

[story 3]

I kept re-reading the confirmation message, the screen reflecting back my own strained expression. It wasn't the rejection that stung the worst; it was the way everyone else seemed to move through the morning as if my existence hadn't registered. I watched as Alex and Ben exchanged easy, congratulatory nods, their shoulders brushing in a gesture of shared success that felt miles away from me. A tightness constricted my throat, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I gripped the edge of my desk until my knuckles turned white, focusing intensely on the grain of the wood, desperate to disappear into the solid reality of the surface beneath my fingertips.
